title: Implement Postgres IndexerBackend
markdown:
## Goal

Implement the first `IndexerBackend` using PostgreSQL. This backend creates a dynamic table based on the `IndexerDefinition`, supports filtering, full-text search, sorting, pagination, and aggregation via SQL.

## Background

The `IndexerBackend` interface from G1-A6-01 defines how indexed data is stored and queried. PostgreSQL is the natural first choice: it has full-text search (tsvector/tsquery), JSON support, and the `PostgresClient` already uses a `SqlExecutor` abstraction that can be reused.

The backend must dynamically create tables based on `IndexerDefinition.fields`. For example, an indexer with fields `[{ name: "title", type: "string", searchable: true }, { name: "rating", type: "number" }]` creates a table with columns `uri TEXT PRIMARY KEY, title TEXT, title_tsvector TSVECTOR, rating NUMERIC, version INTEGER, indexed_at TIMESTAMPTZ`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `PostgresIndexerBackend` class implements `IndexerBackend`
- [ ] Constructor accepts `SqlExecutor` (same pattern as PostgresClient) and `IndexerDefinition`
- [ ] `initialize()` creates a table named `idx_{definition.name}` with:
  - `uri TEXT PRIMARY KEY`
  - Dynamic columns based on `IndexerDefinition.fields` with appropriate SQL types
  - `_tsvector TSVECTOR` for searchable fields (auto-populated via trigger or manual)
  - `version INTEGER`
  - `indexed_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()`
  - Appropriate indexes on indexed fields
  - GIN index on tsvector for full-text search
- [ ] `index(uri, fields, version?)` upserts a row (INSERT ON CONFLICT UPDATE)
- [ ] `remove(uri)` deletes the row
- [ ] `query(IndexQuery)` translates filter operators to SQL WHERE clauses, handles sort/limit/offset
- [ ] `search(text, options?)` uses PostgreSQL `to_tsquery` for full-text search
- [ ] `aggregate(pipeline)` translates aggregation stages to SQL GROUP BY, HAVING, ORDER BY, LIMIT
- [ ] `health()` returns document count and last indexed timestamp
- [ ] `teardown()` drops the table
- [ ] `cleanup()` releases executor resources
- [ ] Tests cover: initialize, index documents, query with filters, full-text search, aggregation, remove, teardown

## Implementation Guide

1. Read `libs/b3nd-indexer/types.ts` for the interfaces
2. Read `libs/b3nd-client-postgres/mod.ts` to understand `SqlExecutor` pattern
3. Create `libs/b3nd-indexer/backends/` directory
4. Create `libs/b3nd-indexer/backends/postgres.ts`:
   - Map `IndexFieldDefinition.type` to SQL types: string->TEXT, number->NUMERIC, boolean->BOOLEAN, date->TIMESTAMPTZ, json->JSONB
   - Build CREATE TABLE SQL dynamically from definition.fields
   - For searchable fields, generate tsvector column and GIN index
   - `index()`: use `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (uri) DO UPDATE SET ...`
   - `query()`: build WHERE clause from filter operators
   - `search()`: use `WHERE _tsvector @@ to_tsquery($1)`
   - `aggregate()`: translate $match/$group/$sort/$limit/$project to SQL
5. Create `libs/b3nd-indexer/backends/postgres_test.ts`
6. Export from `libs/b3nd-indexer/mod.ts`

## Files to Create/Modify

- `libs/b3nd-indexer/backends/postgres.ts` -- new: PostgresIndexerBackend implementation
- `libs/b3nd-indexer/backends/postgres_test.ts` -- new: integration tests
- `libs/b3nd-indexer/mod.ts` -- add export for PostgresIndexerBackend

## Testing

```bash
# Requires a running PostgreSQL instance
deno test --allow-all libs/b3nd-indexer/backends/postgres_test.ts
```

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: G1-A6-01 (core types)
- **Blocks**: G1-A6-03 (processor needs a backend to write to), G1-A6-06 (query tests use backend), G1-A6-07 (aggregation tests)
tasksExcerpt:
- [ ] Implement `IndexerDefinition` interface and runtime
- [ ] Implement `IndexerBackend` — at least one backend (SQLite or Postgres)
- [ ] Implement `createIndexerProcessor` — compose `Processor` that pipes messages to indexer
- [ ] Implement message feed / event stream (pull-based cursor, `GET /api/v1/feed?since={cursor}`)
- [ ] Implement backfill mechanism (paginate through source node, replay through indexer)
- [ ] Implement cursor store (using B3nd node itself as storage)
- [ ] Implement `
